records.ety: [F., from LL. subterfugium, fr. L. subterfugere to flee secretly, to escape; subter under + fugere to flee. See Fugitive.]

defs: That to which one resorts for escape or concealment; an artifice employed to escape censure or the force of an argument, or to justify opinions or conduct; a shift; an evasion.
quotes: Affect not little shifts and subterfuges, to avoid the force of an argument.
By a miserable subterfuge, they hope to render this position safe by rendering it nugatory.
